steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The decisive clue is the opening “but” clause: “Mineralogical differences are detectable in samples... but such differences may not indicate substantial compositional variations in the asteroid.” Observable local differences exist, yet they don't necessarily reveal asteroid-wide differences. This paradox is resolved if the small-scale mineral distribution is not the same everywhere.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
”Uniform” means evenly or consistently distributed throughout. If the distribution is unlikely to be uniform, the minerals are scattered inconsistently—which is why differences are detectable. The other choices don't apply: “neglected” and “redundant” don't describe distributions, and “ongoing” suggests a process rather than a spatial pattern.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
